title: Postel Compliance Vector
description: Canonical schema for @postel/compliance test vectors. Vectors are authored as YAML files under compliance/vectors/<category>/<id>.yaml; the in-memory structure after parsing (YAML or JSON) is what this schema validates. The schema is itself a CONTRACT artifact governed by the compliance capability spec — every conformant runner agrees on this shape. Per the spec's `Vector file schema` requirement (openspec/specs/compliance/spec.md).

id.description: Stable test identifier of the form <category>/<vector-id> (e.g., 'signature-v1/tampered-body').

requirement.description: The CONTRACT requirement this vector covers. Both fields verbatim-match a `### Requirement: <title>` block in openspec/specs/<capability>/spec.md.

title.description: Requirement title, verbatim from the capability spec (no leading '### Requirement:' prefix).

description.description: Human-readable one-line summary of the vector's intent.

input.description: The HTTP request the runner sends to the target.

url.description: Path or absolute URL. Relative paths resolve against --target. May contain {{now±duration}} time templates.

headers.description: Request headers. Values are strings; the runner resolves {{now±duration}} templates in each value before sending.

body_b64.description: Base64-encoded raw request body bytes. Preserves byte-exactness across runners. Empty string is allowed (sends an empty body).

secrets.description: References to test secret/key fixtures used by the vector. Required when signature_mode is 'computed'; optional otherwise.

id.description: Logical name for this secret within the vector (e.g., 'primary', 'verifying'). The first entry is used to compute signatures.

fixture.description: Filename of a key fixture under compliance/vectors/_keys/ (e.g., 'hmac_primary.yaml').

signature_mode.description: 'static' = the signature in input.headers.webhook-signature is pre-computed at vector-authoring time and frozen. 'computed' = the runner resolves time templates, then computes the HMAC or Ed25519 signature using the first secrets[].fixture, then injects it into the outgoing request.

expected.description: The verdict the runner asserts against the target's HTTP response.

outcome.description: 'accept' = target should HTTP 2xx. 'reject' = target should HTTP 4xx with the structured error_code in the X-Postel-Verify-Error header or JSON body.

error_code.description: Structured error code from the receiver capability spec's verify-error vocabulary. Required when outcome is 'reject'; forbidden when outcome is 'accept'.
